steps.title: Use regression for data set B
steps.content: This is a line-of-best-fit question, so the fastest reliable method is a Desmos table regression. From the scatterplot and the new point, data set B is approximately $(2,12)$, $(3,22)$, $(4,28)$, $(5,40)$, $(6,46)$, and $(9,52)$.

steps.title: Enter the table in Desmos
steps.content: Create a table with the $x$-values in $x_1$ and the weights in $y_1$, then run the custom regression $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos reports $m\approx 5.82$.

steps.title: Compare to the choices
steps.content: The slope $s$ of the new best-fit line is about $5.82$, so the closest answer choice is $5.9$.

steps.title: Check the direction
steps.content: This also makes sense without calculation: the new point $(9,52)$ is far below the old line's prediction $-5.6+8.8(9)=73.6$, so the new slope should be much less than $8.8$. Choice A is correct.
